columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The number and percentage of patients experiencing all-cause adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) is reported as 80 (16%). This is derived from the text on page 6, "Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity." The total number of patients in the SOC + AAP group is 501 (Table 1, page 5), so 16% of 501 is approximately 80 patients. While "all-cause" is not explicitly stated, "toxicity" in this general context typically refers to all-cause.
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment

The total number of patients in the SOC-alone group is 502 (Table 1, page 5), so 16% of 502 is approximately 80 patients. While "all-cause" is not explicitly stated, "toxicity" in this general context typically refers to all-cause.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The document reports "grade 3 or higher toxicity" (page 6) and "worst reported grade of toxicity" (page 8), but does not specify if these adverse events were "treatment-related". Therefore, this specific information is not reported.

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The document states "no grade 4 or 5 toxicity" for the 2-year data (page 8), which would imply no grade 5 events. However, this statement refers to "worst reported grade of toxicity" (not specified as treatment-related) and is for the 2-year follow-up. The 4-year data on page 6 mentions "grade 3 or higher toxicity" but does not specify grade 5 or treatment-related. Therefore, specific treatment-related grade 5 data is not reported.
